headline: Transcript: What are the hedge funds shorting?
summary: Katie Martin talks to Robin Wigglesworth
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Unhedged podcast episode: ‘What are the hedge funds shorting?’

Katie MartinThe mood in markets right now remains decidedly wobbly. Stocks have generally had a pretty good year after some early shocks, but now, up at these heights they’re finding the air a little thin. The big worry, of course, is an AI bubble. Are we in one? And if we are, when does it pop, and what happens next?

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Regular listeners will know we’ve talked about this a lot lately, and spoiler alert: we are very likely to keep on talking about it too. But today on the show, we’re trying to find out what the supposedly smartest people in the room are thinking. Heaven knows that’s not us, but we wanna know what are the hedge funds betting against, and what does that mean for the rest of us mere mortals?

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is Unhedged, the markets and finance podcast from the Financial Times and Pushkin. I’m Katie Martin, a markets columnist at the FT in London, where
